title: Run an evidence-bound observe-connector canary
goal: Decide whether a separate narrow observe connector improves least-privilege operation or tool selection enough to justify a second registered connector, using a bounded A/B canary rather than assuming that tool count implies context loading cost.

acceptance.assertion: The canary starts only if T044 identifies a plausible unresolved least-privilege or tool-choice advantage; otherwise the task closes as not justified with evidence.
The canary connector exposes only an explicitly reviewed observe profile and cannot mutate, reveal secrets, signal processes, deploy, merge or silently route to the trusted-owner connector.
The same golden prompts are run against one-connector and two-connector configurations with recorded server, publication profile, model and client snapshot identity where observable.

The comparison measures correct tool or connector selection, refusal when authority is insufficient, unnecessary escalation, latency and observable context or schema cost without treating JSON bytes as model tokens.
The decision includes configuration drift, deployment, refresh, naming ambiguity, maintenance and incident-response cost of a second connector.
A second default connector is adopted only with a material repeatable benefit and no unacceptable ambiguity; otherwise the canary is removed and the single-connector profile model remains canonical.
The closeout does not claim automatic context savings when deferred tool discovery is active unless direct client-side evidence proves them.
